recipes.title: Arepa
recipes.description: Venezuelan griddled cornmeal cake split and filled with cheese, meats or shredded beef.

ingredients.name: queso de mano or fresh white cheese

steps: Mix water and salt, gradually add corn flour and oil, kneading into a smooth, soft dough.
Rest the dough 5 minutes, then divide into 6 portions and shape into 2 cm thick discs.
Heat a budare or heavy pan over medium heat and cook arepas 4-5 minutes per side until a crust forms.
Transfer to a 200°C oven for 10 minutes until they sound hollow when tapped.
Split open and fill with cheese or your filling of choice.

recipes.title: Pabellón Criollo
recipes.description: Venezuelan national plate of shredded beef, black beans, white rice and fried sweet plantain.
steps: Simmer the flank steak in water with onion and salt for 90 minutes until very tender, then shred.
Sauté onion, bell pepper and garlic with cumin, add tomatoes and the shredded beef and cook until thickened.
Cook the rice with salt and a splash of oil until fluffy.
Reheat the black beans seasoned with sautéed garlic, onion and a touch of cumin.
Slice plantains diagonally and pan-fry in oil until caramelised on both sides.
Plate rice, beef, beans and plantain side by side.

recipes.title: Hallaca
recipes.description: Venezuelan Christmas tamale of corn dough filled with spiced beef, pork and chicken stew, wrapped in plantain leaves.
steps: Brown the meats with onion, leek and bell pepper, season with salt, cumin and a splash of wine; simmer 1 hour and chill overnight.
Knead corn flour with warm stock and annatto lard until a soft, pliable, deeply yellow dough forms.
Pass plantain leaves over a flame to soften, then cut into 30 cm rectangles and oil lightly.
Spread a thin disc of dough on each leaf, top with stewed meats, olives, capers and raisins.
Fold the leaf into a tight rectangular parcel and tie with twine in a cross pattern.
Boil hallacas in salted water for 45 minutes, drain and rest before serving.

recipes.title: Cachapa
recipes.description: Venezuelan sweet fresh-corn pancake folded around queso de mano, cooked on a griddle.

ingredients.name: queso de mano or fresh mozzarella

steps: Blend corn, milk, sugar, salt and corn flour until thick but pourable.
Melt a little butter on a hot griddle and pour about a cup of batter, spreading into a 20 cm round.
Cook 3-4 minutes until set and lightly browned, then flip and cook another 2-3 minutes.
Place a generous slice of cheese on one half, fold over and serve immediately with extra butter.

recipes.title: Tequeños
recipes.description: Venezuelan party finger food of soft white cheese batons wrapped in wheat dough and deep-fried.

ingredients.name: queso blanco or halloumi, cut into batons

steps: Mix flour, sugar and salt, rub in butter, then add egg and water to form a smooth elastic dough; rest 30 minutes.
Roll dough thin and cut long strips 2 cm wide.
Wrap each cheese baton spirally with a strip of dough, sealing the ends with water.
Heat oil to 170°C and fry tequeños in batches for 3-4 minutes until deeply golden.
Drain on paper and serve hot with guasacaca or pink sauce.

recipes.title: Asado Negro
recipes.description: Caracas Sunday classic of beef eye round slow-braised in caramelised sugar, wine and aromatics until jet-black and tender.
steps: Marinate the beef overnight with crushed garlic, Worcestershire, salt and pepper.
In a heavy pot, melt the sugar with a splash of oil until it forms a deep mahogany caramel.
Add the beef and sear it on all sides in the caramel until blackened.
Add onion, leek and garlic; deglaze with red wine, then add stock and bay leaf.
Cover and braise on low heat for 2.5 hours, turning the meat every 30 minutes.
Remove the meat, blend the sauce smooth, slice the beef and nap with the dark gravy.

recipes.title: Pernil
recipes.description: Venezuelan slow-roasted pork leg marinated in garlic, citrus and herbs, a Christmas table centrepiece.
steps: Score the pork skin in a diamond pattern.
Blend garlic, citrus juices, oregano, soy, Worcestershire, oil, salt and pepper into a marinade.
Pierce the meat and rub the marinade deep into all crevices; refrigerate at least 12 hours.
Roast at 160°C, covered, for 4 hours, basting every hour.
Uncover, raise heat to 220°C and roast 30-40 minutes until skin is crackling.
Rest 20 minutes before carving and serve with the pan juices.

recipes.title: Pisca Andina
recipes.description: Venezuelan Andean breakfast soup of potato, milk, cilantro, egg and white cheese.
steps: Sauté onion and garlic in butter until soft.
Add potatoes and water and simmer until potatoes are tender, about 20 minutes.
Pour in the milk and most of the cilantro and bring back to a gentle simmer.
Crack the eggs straight into the soup and poach for 3-4 minutes until set.
Ladle into bowls, top with crumbled cheese and the remaining cilantro.

recipes.title: Chupe Andino
recipes.description: Andean Venezuelan creamy chowder of chicken, potato, corn and queso blanco.
steps: Simmer chicken with onion, leek and water for 40 minutes; remove, shred and reserve the broth.
Sauté garlic in oil and add the broth, potatoes, carrot and corn; cook until potatoes are tender.
Return shredded chicken and pour in the milk; simmer gently 10 minutes.
Stir in cheese and cilantro just before serving so the cheese stays soft.

recipes.title: Mandoca
recipes.description: Maracaibo street snack of ring-shaped fried dough made from corn, plantain, panela and white cheese.
steps: Boil plantains until soft, then mash.
Dissolve panela in warm water, add salt and anise.
Mix mashed plantain, corn flour, cheese and panela water into a soft dough; rest 10 minutes.
Roll pieces into 15 cm ropes and join the ends to form rings.
Heat oil to 170°C and fry mandocas until deep golden, about 4 minutes.
Drain and serve hot with butter or fresh cheese.

recipes.title: Carne Mechada
recipes.description: Venezuelan shredded beef braised with sofrito, peppers and tomato, the classic filling for arepas and pabellón.
steps: Cover the beef with water, add a halved onion, salt and bay leaf, and simmer 90 minutes until very tender.
Cool slightly and shred the beef along the grain; reserve a cup of cooking liquid.
Sauté onion, bell peppers and garlic in oil until softened.
Add tomatoes and cumin, cook 5 minutes, then stir in the shredded beef, Worcestershire and reserved liquid.
Simmer 15 minutes until the sauce coats the beef; adjust salt and serve.

recipes.title: Polvorosa
recipes.description: Venezuelan double-crusted chicken pie with a delicate, crumbly butter pastry and a sweet-savoury filling.
steps: Rub flour, sugar, salt and butter into a coarse crumb, bind with yolks and a splash of water; chill 1 hour.
Sauté onion until soft, add tomato, chicken, raisins, olives and Worcestershire and cook 10 minutes; cool.
Roll two-thirds of the dough into a 24 cm pie pan.
Fill with the chicken mixture, top with the remaining rolled dough and seal the edges.
Brush with egg yolk and bake at 180°C for 35-40 minutes until pale gold and crisp.
